title: Preserved Bloombase news index capture
summary: A local preserved copy of the Bloombase news-and-events index turns the release trail itself into evidence. Instead of citing the company chronology abstractly, the chronicle can now show the index page that exposes the 2012 → 2025 sequence in one place.

metrics.label: Commented-out 2017 Huawei entry preserved in source

notes: Useful because it proves the chronology is not assembled only from scattered direct URLs; the index itself shows the release cadence on a first-party page.

The commented-out Huawei 2017 block remains visible in the preserved HTML, which is valuable for explaining why the site keeps that source as supporting chronology even though it is not rendered live on the page.

entryHighlights.title: Open Virtualization Alliance overview by Bloombase
entryHighlights.summary: Shows the bridge source that predates the denser first-party release run.

entryHighlights.title: Bloombase joins OpenStack community
entryHighlights.summary: Where the founder-era chronology becomes direct and company-hosted.

entryHighlights.title: Marvell Teams with Bloombase for LiquidSecurity Network HSM
entryHighlights.summary: Important because the first-party index itself points out to a partner-hosted press release, thickening the trust-layer story.

entryHighlights.title: Bloombase and Utimaco announce technology alliance for the AI era
entryHighlights.summary: Extends the same chronology surface into the present-tense AI chapter.
